easy.title: Sentence Transformation: Present and past
easy.instructions: Finish the second sentence so that it means the same as the first. Use between two and five words, including the key word.

items.sentence1: I last saw him in May.
items.explanation: Present perfect: haven't seen (from a point in the past until now).

items.sentence1: She started learning English three years ago.
items.explanation: Present perfect continuous: has been learning (duration).

items.sentence1: The meeting is at 9 o'clock.
items.explanation: Same meaning: is at = starts at.

items.sentence1: It's possible she forgot.
items.explanation: Might have + past participle for past possibility.

items.sentence1: I regret not studying harder.
items.explanation: Wish + past perfect for regret about the past.

medium.title: Sentence Transformation: Grammar focus
medium.instructions: Finish the second sentence so that it means the same as the first. Use between two and five words, including the key word.

items.sentence1: They built the house in 1990.
items.explanation: Passive: was built (past simple passive).

items.sentence1: She is too young to drive.
items.explanation: Too young = not old enough.

items.sentence1: I don't know the answer.
items.explanation: Wish + past simple for present unreal wish.

items.sentence1: He said: 'I will help you.'
items.explanation: Reported speech: pronoun and tense change.

items.sentence1: We couldn't go out because of the rain.
items.explanation: Prevent someone from doing something.

hard.title: Sentence Transformation: Advanced
hard.instructions: Finish the second sentence so that it means the same as the first. Use between two and five words, including the key word.

items.sentence1: It's a pity you didn't tell me earlier.
items.explanation: Wish + past perfect for regret (you didn't tell).

items.sentence1: Nobody had invited her to the party.
items.explanation: Past perfect passive: hadn't been invited.

items.sentence1: The film was so boring that we left.
items.explanation: So boring (adj) = such a boring (noun).

items.sentence1: She asked me: 'Have you finished?'
items.explanation: Reported yes/no question: if + subject + had.

items.sentence1: I've never been to Japan.
items.explanation: First time + present perfect.

expert.title: Sentence Transformation: Expert
expert.instructions: Finish the second sentence so that it means the same as the first. Use between two and five words, including the key word.

items.sentence1: They say he escaped from prison.
items.explanation: Passive reporting: is said to have + past participle.

items.sentence1: Despite the cost, we decided to go.
items.explanation: Although + clause (same meaning as despite + noun).

items.sentence1: I last saw her at the conference.
items.explanation: Present perfect: haven't seen her since.

items.sentence1: She might not have received the message.
items.explanation: Might not have = it's possible (that) she didn't.

items.sentence1: The project will be completed by Friday.
items.explanation: Future perfect: will have completed (by Friday).
